---
title: "Verification: Remove Sparse GRM for Inbred Mappings"
date: 2026-04-27
categories: [verification]
status: in-progress
branch: "fix/rm-sparse"
pr: 0
---

For inbred mappings (`--fastGWA-mlm-exact`), the pipeline previously built a sparse GRM
approximation via `gcta64 --make-bK-sparse 0.05` and passed it with `--grm-sparse`. This branch
removes that intermediate step and passes the full GRM directly with `--grm`, consistent with
how the loco path (`--mlma-loco`) already behaves. The `sparse_cut` parameter is removed from
the pipeline entirely.

Files changed: `modules/gcta/perform_gwa/main.nf`, `main.nf`, `nextflow.config`,
`nextflow_schema.json`.

---

## Summary Checklist

Fill in the **Result** column as you work through each step.

| # | Step | Environment | Pass criterion | Result |
|---|------|-------------|----------------|--------|
| 0 | `NXF_VER=24.10.5` pinned | local + HPC | `nextflow -version` shows 24.10.x | [ ] |
| 1a | stub-run `test` | local | exits 0, no `No such variable: sparse_cut` | [ ] |
| 1b | stub-run `test,docker` | local | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 1c | stub-run `test_variable` | local | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 1d | stub-run `test_cv_pool` | local | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 1e | stub-run `test_three_species` | local | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 2a | stub-run `test_hpc,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 2b | stub-run `test_hpc_variable,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 2c | stub-run `test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 2d | stub-run `test_hpc_three_species,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 3a | `Rscript tests/run_tests.R` | local | all pass/skip | [ ] |
| 4a | full run `test` + integration tests | local | all tests pass | [ ] |
| 4b | full run `test_variable` + tests | local | all tests pass | [ ] |
| 4c | no sparse GRM files in work dir | local | `find` returns nothing | [ ] |
| 4d | inbred p-value sanity check | local R | all finite, range plausible | [ ] |
| 5a | HPC stub-run `test_hpc,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | [ ] |
| 5b | HPC full run `test_hpc,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0, assessment TSV present | [ ] |
| 5c | HPC full run `test_hpc_variable,rockfish` | Rockfish | exits 0 | [ ] |

---

## 0. Nextflow version pinning

The pipeline requires Nextflow `>=24.10.0,<25.0.0`. Pin the version before every run.

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow -version   # must show 24.10.x
```

::: {.callout-warning}
If `nextflow -version` reports 25.x, stub-run publish-block warnings will appear. These are
pre-existing on machines with system Nextflow ≥ 25.x and unrelated to this change — set
`NXF_VER=24.10.5` anyway to keep the output clean.
:::

---

## 1. Pipeline wiring — stub-runs (local)

Stub-runs confirm that Nextflow resolves every `input:` / `output:` declaration without running
any computation. The critical check here is that removing `val sparse_cut` from `GCTA_PERFORM_GWA`
and dropping the corresponding `params.sparse_cut` argument from `main.nf` does not leave a
dangling reference anywhere in the channel graph.

### 1a. Fixed architecture (`test`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria:** exits 0; no `No such variable: sparse_cut` or `Missing output file` errors.
The `GCTA_PERFORM_GWA` stubs for both `inbred` and `loco` modes complete successfully.

### 1b. Fixed architecture with Docker (`test,docker`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test,docker -stub-run
```

### 1c. Variable architecture (`test_variable`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_variable -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria:** exits 0 across all combinations in the cartesian product.

### 1d. Non-marker CV pool (`test_cv_pool`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_cv_pool -stub-run
```

### 1e. Three-species path (`test_three_species`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_three_species -stub-run
```

---

## 2. Pipeline wiring — stub-runs (HPC)

Run on Rockfish. Confirms the SLURM + Singularity wiring resolves correctly without `sparse_cut`.

### 2a. HPC fixed architecture (`test_hpc,rockfish`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc,rockfish -stub-run
```

### 2b. HPC variable architecture (`test_hpc_variable,rockfish`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish -stub-run
```

### 2c. HPC non-marker CV pool (`test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish -stub-run
```

### 2d. HPC three-species (`test_hpc_three_species,rockfish`)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc_three_species,rockfish -stub-run
```

**Pass criteria for all HPC stub-runs:** exits 0; no SLURM submission errors.

---

## 3. R unit and schema tests

No R library files were modified by this branch, so only the standard test suite is needed.

```bash
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

**Pass criteria:** all tests pass or skip cleanly (integration tests auto-skip when
`TEST_DB_DIR` is unset). No failures related to `sparse_cut` or `GCTA_PERFORM_GWA` signatures.

---

## 4. Integration tests — local pipeline run

Runs the full pipeline with Docker and validates the resulting database. The key checks beyond
the standard suite are: (a) no sparse GRM intermediate files appear in the work directory, and
(b) inbred mapping p-values are finite and plausible.

### 4a. Fixed architecture

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test
```

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Result:
```
```

### 4b. Variable architecture

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable
```

```bash
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=256 \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

Result:
```
```

### 4c. Confirm no sparse GRM intermediate files

The old code emitted `{prefix}_sparse_grm_{mode}.grm.{bin,N.bin,id}` into the work directory.
After this change, no such files should exist.

```bash
# Adjust the work dir path to match your local run
find Analysis_Results-*/work -name "*_sparse_grm_*" 2>/dev/null
# Expected: no output (exits 0 with empty result)
```

**Pass criteria:** the `find` command returns no files.

### 4d. Inbred p-value sanity check

Confirms that `fastGWA-mlm-exact` with `--grm` (full GRM) produces valid, finite p-values across
all inbred mapping tasks. Adjusts `db_dir` to match the output from Step 4a.

```r
#| eval: false
library(arrow)
library(dplyr)

db_dir <- "tests/integration_data/test/db"

# Read all mappings metadata and join to mapping data for inbred tasks
meta <- read_parquet(file.path(db_dir, "mappings_metadata.parquet")) |>
  filter(mode == "inbred")

cat("Inbred mapping count:", nrow(meta), "\n")
stopifnot(nrow(meta) > 0)

# Load p-values from one inbred mapping partition (spot-check)
mapping_id <- meta$mapping_id[1]
pop        <- meta$population[1]

mapping_file <- file.path(
  db_dir, "mappings",
  paste0("population=", pop),
  paste0("mapping_id=", mapping_id),
  "data.parquet"
)
d <- read_parquet(mapping_file)

cat("Rows:", nrow(d), "\n")
cat("P range: [", min(d$P), ",", max(d$P), "]\n")
cat("Any NA P:", any(is.na(d$P)), "\n")
cat("Any infinite P:", any(!is.finite(d$P)), "\n")

stopifnot(
  nrow(d) > 0,
  all(!is.na(d$P)),
  all(is.finite(d$P)),
  all(d$P >= 0),
  all(d$P <= 1)
)
cat("PASS: inbred p-values are finite and in [0, 1]\n")
```

---

## 5. Integration tests — HPC pipeline run (Rockfish)

Run on Rockfish after all local checks pass.

### 5a. Stub-run (already covered in Step 2a, re-confirm if needed)

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf -profile test_hpc,rockfish -stub-run
```

### 5b. Full HPC run — fixed architecture

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-rm-sparse-verification \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/${USER}/nf-work-rm-sparse
```

Result:
```
Completed at:
Duration    :
CPU hours   :
Succeeded   :
```

**Pass criteria:** exits 0; `db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv` present; inbred mapping files
(`*_lmm-exact_inbred_*.fastGWA`) present in published output; assessment metrics are in a
plausible range (power and precision not degenerate).

### 5c. Full HPC run — variable architecture

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-rm-sparse-variable \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/${USER}/nf-work-rm-sparse-variable
```

Result:
```
Completed at:
Duration    :
CPU hours   :
Succeeded   :
```

---

## Notes

<!-- Record any issues, unexpected behavior, or deviations from the standard workflow. -->

<!-- Key design note: fastGWA-mlm-exact supports both --grm (full GRM) and --grm-sparse.
     The sparse step was removed to eliminate an extra GCTA invocation per task and to make
     the inbred path symmetric with the loco path, which never used --grm-sparse.
     The LOCO comment in perform_gwa/main.nf (that --mlma-loco requires the full GRM) was
     already accurate; this branch removes the discrepancy on the inbred side. -->
